addressedFeedback.finding: Step 3 accepted the TypeScript deletion-readiness design with no high or mid findings.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Created a focused active implementation plan that creates and validates a blocked deletion-readiness gate instead of deleting TypeScript.

addressedFeedback.finding: PROGRESS.json task metadata and README indexing drifted from the current implementation plan.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Aligned PROGRESS.json to the current five-task plan, removed duplicate README entry, and corrected TASK-004 parallelization/status notes.

addressedFeedback.finding: Accepted implementation plan required the first bounded TypeScript deletion-readiness TODO-loop slice rather than TypeScript deletion.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Created a tracked deletion gate, deterministic Swift validator/tests, separate Homebrew metadata reference, progress updates, and verification evidence while preserving TypeScript/Bun source, tests, commands, and fallback documentation.

addressedFeedback.finding: validate(_:) reported blocked domains but did not mark nil evidenceCommands or evidenceArtifacts as invalid for directly constructed gate models.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added deterministic missing evidenceCommands/evidenceArtifacts diagnostics in SwiftDeletionReadinessValidator.validate(_:) and a focused Swift regression; SwiftDeletionReadinessTests now pass 7 tests.

addressedFeedback.finding: The deletion-readiness gate stored host-local codex-agent evidence artifact paths.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Replaced host-local paths in packaging/swift-deletion-readiness.json, impl-plans/completed/swift-deletion-readiness-gate.md, and PROGRESS.json with portable external-reference:codex-agent/... references.

addressedFeedback.finding: TASK-003 claimed CLI WorkflowCommands.swift and WorkflowCommandTests.swift deliverables were completed although those files were unchanged.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Revised the implementation plan and PROGRESS task metadata to state TASK-003 is packaging/test visibility only and updated diff-check verification paths accordingly.

addressedFeedback.finding: Validator could report TypeScript deletion ready from self-attested status/review labels, placeholder evidence arrays, and any lastVerifiedAt value.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added SwiftDeletionReadinessValidationContext and require matching verifiedBranch/verifiedCommit, acceptedReviewWorkflowId, acceptedReviewNodeId, and no high/mid acceptedReviewFindingSeverities before allowsTypeScriptDeletion can validate true; added stale-commit and high/mid-review regressions.

addressedFeedback.finding: Tracked gate test decoded JSON and called validate(_:) directly, bypassing structural key-presence checks from decodeAndValidate(_:).
addressedFeedback.resolution: Changed the primary tracked gate test to validate raw JSON through decodeAndValidate(_:) and added missing lastVerifiedAt/notes regressions.

addressedFeedback.finding: SwiftDeletionReadinessValidationResult.allowsTypeScriptDeletion could be true even when diagnostics made the gate invalid, including duplicate domains or missing required structural fields.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Made decodeAndValidate(_:, context:) and validate(_:, context:) require diagnostics.isEmpty before allowsTypeScriptDeletion can be true; added duplicate-domain and missing-notes deletion-ready regressions.

addressedFeedback.finding: Repository diff still contained out-of-scope deleted skill files and unrelated Swift session-indexing, GraphQL, CLI, design, flake, script, and test changes.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Restored tracked out-of-scope files to HEAD, removed untracked out-of-scope session-indexing files, and cleaned impl-plans/README.md plus impl-plans/PROGRESS.json so Step 6 diff is scoped to deletion-readiness gate files only.

addressedFeedback.finding: Deletion-ready validation context only bound branch and commit while acceptedReviewWorkflowId and acceptedReviewNodeId were accepted as any non-empty strings.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Extended SwiftDeletionReadinessValidationContext with expected acceptedReviewWorkflowId and expectedAcceptedReviewNodeId, require exact matches in diagnostics and blocking checks, and added bogus workflow/node regressions.

addressedFeedback.finding: Deletion-ready validation only blocked severities normalized exactly to high or mid, so medium, critical, blocker, unknown, or blank severity labels could be treated as non-blocking accepted review evidence.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added explicit accepted-review severity classification: low/info/informational/none are non-blocking, high/mid/medium/critical/blocker block deletion readiness, and unknown or blank labels are invalid and blocking; added regressions for medium, critical, blocker, unknown, and blank labels.

addressedFeedback.finding: Deletion-ready validation treated any non-empty evidenceCommands/evidenceArtifacts and any non-empty lastVerifiedAt as sufficient, allowing placeholder commands, source-only artifacts, malformed timestamps, or empty acceptedReviewFindingSeverities to unlock deletion readiness.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Require parseable ISO-8601 lastVerifiedAt, reject placeholder evidence commands, require durable evidence artifact references such as workflow-artifact:, verification-result:, or /tmp/riela-artifact-dev/ paths, and require explicit non-empty non-blocking accepted-review severity evidence; added focused regressions for each bypass.

addressedFeedback.finding: Deletion-ready validation accepted syntactically durable artifact references without resolving command-result metadata or binding evidence contents to the current commit and accepted review.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added SwiftDeletionReadinessEvidenceArtifact metadata and require every deletion-ready evidence artifact to resolve with exitCode 0, matching domain id, listed command, branch, commit, workflow id, and review node id; added unresolved and failed-command regressions.

addressedFeedback.finding: Validator required migrationStatus to remain incomplete even when allowsTypeScriptDeletion validated true, creating contradictory deletion-ready state.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Require migrationStatus=deletion_ready when allowsTypeScriptDeletion and typeScriptSourceDeletionReady are both true, while tracked blocked gates remain migrationStatus=incomplete; added contradictory-status regression.

addressedFeedback.finding: Implementation plan referenced design-docs/specs/design-swift-native-migration.md#typescript-deletion-readiness-todo-loop, but the design document did not contain that accepted deletion-readiness TODO-loop anchor or gate terms.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added the TypeScript Deletion-Readiness TODO Loop section to design-docs/specs/design-swift-native-migration.md with swift-deletion-readiness.json, blocked deletion flags, required parity domains, codex-agent reference rules, and durable accepted-evidence requirements.

addressedFeedback.finding: Deletion-ready validation could accept a domain when every resolved artifact was valid but not every listed evidence command had a matching successful artifact.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Require every deletion-ready evidence command to have at least one resolved successful evidence artifact bound to the same domain id, branch, commit, workflow id, and review node id; added a multi-command missing-artifact regression.

addressedFeedback.finding: Deletion-ready validation could accept irrelevant non-placeholder commands as parity evidence for package, CLI, server, GraphQL, event, workflow package, persistence, release, documentation, test, and agent domains.
addressedFeedback.resolution: Added domain-specific required evidence command groups and updated deletion-ready fixtures to require matching Swift parity plus TypeScript/Bun, release, or documentation commands; added a validator-only command regression.

codexAgentReference: ../../codex-agent unavailable; external-reference:codex-agent is reference-only. Riela TypeScript adapters and pinned package contracts remain authoritative for deletion-readiness parity.
issueReference: workflow input issue title: Swift migration TODO loop until TypeScript can be deleted; no issueUrl or issueNumber supplied
parallelization: TASK-001 is the only task parallelizable at plan start.
TASK-002 is sequential after TASK-001 because it validates the actual gate schema.
TASK-003 is sequential after TASK-002 because CLI or packaging visibility must consume validator results rather than duplicate validation.
TASK-004 is sequential after TASK-001 so progress metadata does not claim implemented evidence before the gate exists.
TASK-005 is sequential because review handoff depends on actual verification evidence.
residualRisks: This plan creates the first bounded gate slice only; it does not complete Swift migration or authorize TypeScript deletion.
Full deletion readiness remains blocked until every required domain has current accepted evidence with no high or mid review findings.
Agent parity for codex-agent, claude-code-agent, and cursor-cli-agent must remain mapped to authoritative TypeScript adapters and pinned package contracts until approved Swift references exist.
CLI or packaging visibility should be skipped or narrowed if no existing local pattern fits without inventing new user-facing architecture.

TASK-001.summary: Create blocked deletion-readiness gate artifact with all required package, CLI, server, GraphQL, event, workflow package, persistence, release, documentation, test, and agent domains

TASK-002.summary: Add deterministic Swift gate model and validator for required fields, required domains, duplicate ids, and unsafe aggregate deletion-ready claims

TASK-003.summary: Expose gate status through packaging readiness metadata and tests without changing production cutover behavior or adding a new CLI command surface

TASK-004.summary: Update completed plan index and progress metadata while keeping full Swift migration and TypeScript deletion readiness false

TASK-005.summary: Run JSON, blocked-state, focused Swift, TypeScript baseline, workflow validation, and diff verification before review handoff

verificationCommands: bun run packages/riela/src/bin.ts workflow validate codex-design-and-implement-review-loop --scope project

verifiedEvidence.adversarialRevisionVerification: Deletion-ready domains require resolved successful command-result metadata bound to artifact ref, domain id, listed command, branch, commit, workflow id, and review node id; every listed evidence command must have matching resolved successful artifact coverage; every domain must include required Swift parity plus TypeScript/Bun, release, or documentation command groups; migrationStatus must be deletion_ready when both deletion flags are true.
commands: bun run packages/riela/src/bin.ts workflow validate codex-design-and-implement-review-loop --scope project
verifiedEvidence.designReferenceVerification: rg -n "^## TypeScript Deletion-Readiness TODO Loop|swift-deletion-readiness\.json|allowsTypeScriptDeletion=false|typeScriptSourceDeletionReady=false|claude-code-agent|codex-agent|cursor-cli-agent" design-docs/specs/design-swift-native-migration.md passed; design-docs/specs/design-swift-native-migration.md#typescript-deletion-readiness-todo-loop exists.
verifiedEvidence.scope: first bounded TypeScript deletion-readiness TODO-loop slice
verifiedEvidence.step7RevisionVerification: Design reference rg check, jq JSON checks, bun lint:biome, bun typecheck:server, workflow validate, and scoped git diff --check passed after Step 7 design-reference fix; prior focused SwiftDeletionReadinessTests 23 tests and SwiftPackagingReadiness cutover test 1 test passed after code changes.
